explicit_text: I was born in Crescent City, California, a small town in northern California. It's where my story begins before my family moved to Seattle. Although I didn't grow up there, Crescent City is significant as my birthplace and the starting point of my journey as part of the Hmong immigrant experience.
implicit_text: I was born in a small town in northern California. It's where my story begins before my family moved to Seattle. Although I didn't grow up there, that northern coastal town is significant as my birthplace and the starting point of my journey as part of the Hmong immigrant experience.

explicit_text: I was raised in Seattle, Washington, and that's where my family still lives today. Growing up there shaped much of my early life and identity. Seattle represents the place where my family settled and where I spent my formative years before moving to St. Paul.
implicit_text: I was raised in a major city in Washington state, and that's where my family still lives today. Growing up there shaped much of my early life and identity. That Pacific Northwest city represents the place where my family settled and where I spent my formative years before moving to St. Paul.

explicit_text: Now, I live in St. Paul, Minnesota, where I also went to school. St. Paul is where I pursued my education and continue to build my life. It’s a place that represents my growth and the next chapter beyond my upbringing in Seattle.
implicit_text: Now, I live in the capital city of Minnesota, where I also went to school. This city is where I pursued my education and continue to build my life. It’s a place that represents my growth and the next chapter beyond my upbringing in Seattle.

explicit_text: I attended Concordia University here in St. Paul, where I furthered my education. Going to Concordia was an important step for me as part of my journey as a Hmong immigrant in the United States. It’s where I gained knowledge and experiences that helped shape who I am today.
implicit_text: I attended a private university located in the capital city of Minnesota, where I furthered my education. Going to this university was an important step for me as part of my journey as a Hmong immigrant in the United States. It’s where I gained knowledge and experiences that helped shape who I am today.
